searchDocs.title: Using VluxySF
searchDocs.content: Using VluxySF Here are some quick links to get started using VluxySF Installation guideAPI DocsInitializationFolder setupExamples

searchDocs.title: 1. Setting Up the Sound Folder​
searchDocs.content: First, create a Configuration (commonly named SOUNDS) containing all your sound instances. For details on organizing this folder, see Folder Setup.

searchDocs.title: 2. Server Initialization​
searchDocs.content: On the server, inject the folder or configuration containing your instanced sounds. This folder should be located in a service like ServerStorage.  local ServerStorage = game:GetService("ServerStorage") local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) -- Reference to the folder/config of the actual sounds you want to use local soundFolder = ServerStorage:FindFirstChild("SOUNDS") -- Initialize the server VluxySF._initServer(soundFolder)

searchDocs.title: 3. Client Initialization​
searchDocs.content: On the client, simply call the initialization function:  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) -- Initialize the client VluxySF._initClient()

searchDocs.title: Wally Configuration​
searchDocs.content: Once Wally is installed, run wally init on your project directory, and then add VluxySF as a dependency. For example, the following could be a wally.toml file for a project that includes a few of these modules:  [package] name = "your_name/your_project" version = "0.1.0" registry = "https://github.com/UpliftGames/wally-index" realm = "shared" [dependencies] VluxySF = "greenviper126/VluxySF@^1"   To install this dependency, run wally install within your project. Wally will create a Package folder in your directory with the installed dependency.

searchDocs.title: Rojo Configuration​
searchDocs.content: The Package folder created by Wally should be synced into Roblox Studio through your Rojo configuration. For instance, a Rojo configuration might have the following entry to sync the Packages folder into ReplicatedStorage:  { "name": "vluxysf", "tree": { "$className": "DataModel", "ReplicatedStorage": { "$className": "ReplicatedStorage", "Packages": { "$path": "Packages" } } } }

searchDocs.title: The Basics: Folder Structure​
searchDocs.content: VluxySF uses your folder structure to automatically group and identify sounds. You do not need to manually assign SoundGroups or create them.

searchDocs.title: Why Structure Matters​
searchDocs.content: A well-organized sound folder means:  Instantly accessible sounds by stringAutomatic grouping for volume/effect controlEasy preloading for lag-free playbackCleaner, more maintainable projects    Legend:  ⚙️ = Configuration Instance 📁 = Folder Instance 🔊 = Sound Instance   Naming Conventions:  UPPER_CASE for SoundGroupsPascalCase for FolderscamelCase for SoundsAvoid spaces and special characters for best compatibility.Sound locations are used as keys for programmatic access (e.g., Sounds.Create("MUSIC/Explosions/explosion1")).    Example:  SOUNDS⚙️ ← Base file ├─ MUSIC⚙️ ← Name of SoundGroup │ ├─ mainTheme🔊 ← Grouped to MUSIC SoundGroup │ └─ battle🔊 ← Grouped to MUSIC SoundGroup └─ SFX⚙️ ← Name of SoundGroup ├─ click🔊 ← Grouped to SFX SoundGroup └─ explosion🔊 ← Grouped to SFX SoundGroup   Music and SFX become SoundGroups, and their children are grouped accordingly.  Tip: Once SoundGroupConfigurations are defined (MUSIC, SFX, etc.), organization within them is your choice. Tip: It is recommended that you only use Folders and Sound Instances while organizing within a defined SoundGroupConfigurations. The only exception is _Preload (which should be a Configuration) and adding SoundEffects to a Sound Instance.

searchDocs.title: Organizing with Subfolders​
searchDocs.content: You can use additional folders (📁) inside SoundGroups to keep your sounds organized. Doing so will effect the location of the sound when fetching.  Example:  SOUNDS⚙️ └─ SFX⚙️ ├─ UI📁 │ └─ click🔊 └─ Game📁 └─ explosion🔊   Tip: Plan your folder organization early. Changing a sound’s location means you must update its path in code too. Tip: You can have the same Sound Instance in different locations. But if they are both direct children of a folder, they must have different names or only one will exist in the SoundDefinitions.

searchDocs.title: Preloading Sounds Automatically​
searchDocs.content: Want certain sounds to be ready instantly? Any folder named _Preload (with the underscore) will have all its sounds (and subfolders) preloaded automatically on the client.  How to use:  SOUNDS⚙️ ├─ MUSIC⚙️ │ ├─ mainTheme🔊 │ └─ battle🔊 └─ _Preload⚙️ <-- all children will be preloaded ├─ importantSound1🔊 └─ importantSound2🔊 └─ SFX⚙️ ├─ click🔊 ├─ explosion🔊 └─ _Preload⚙️ <-- all children will be preloaded │ ├─ importantSound1🔊 │ └─ importantSound2🔊 └─ Explosions📁 ├─ unimportantSound1🔊 └─ unimportantSound2🔊 └─ _Preload⚙️ <-- all children will be preloaded ├─ importantSound1🔊 └─ importantSound2🔊   Note: You can have multiple _Preload folders in different SoundGroups if needed. Note: This works similarly to Unity's Resources Folder.

searchDocs.title: Preload Timeout​
searchDocs.content: You can set an optional timeout (in seconds) for preloading. If preloading takes too long, it will continue in the background. Default is 5 seconds.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) local preloadTimeout = 10 VluxySF._initClient(preloadTimeout)

searchDocs.title: Configuring Sound Instances​
searchDocs.content: Each Sound instance can be customized with any Roblox sound properties and child sound effects:  Properties: Set properties like SoundId, Volume, PlaybackSpeed, etc., directly on the Sound instance.Effects: Add child instances such as EqualizerSoundEffect, ReverbSoundEffect, etc, to the Sound for automatic serialization and reconstruction.  Adding any children that are not Roblox SoundEffects to a Sound in the SOUNDS config will not be serialized.

searchDocs.title: End Result​
searchDocs.content: A SOUNDS Config should look something like this when you’re done in Roblox Studio:

searchDocs.title: Basic Playback (Client/Server)​
searchDocs.content: Fetch and play a sound instance by name. This works on both client and server.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() -- Wait until schema is loaded local sound = VluxySF.ConstructSoundFromName("MUSIC/LoFi/beat1") -- waits until sound is loaded if sound then sound:Play() end   "Music/LoFi/Beat1" is just an example path in your SOUNDS folder.

searchDocs.title: Manipulating SoundGroup Volumes​
searchDocs.content: Set the SoundGroup volumes at the start of a session. This can be done on server or client (client is recommended for user settings).  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local masterVolume = 0.8 local subVolumes = { ["GAME"] = 0.7, ["MUSIC"] = 0.55, ["SFX"] = 0.64, } local function UpdateSoundGroupVolumes() for _, soundGroup in ipairs(VluxySF.GetSoundGroups()) do local rawVolume = subVolumes[soundGroup.Name] or 0.5 local realVolume = rawVolume * masterVolume soundGroup.Volume = realVolume end end UpdateSoundGroupVolumes()   Tip: You can call this function whenever a slider or setting changes to update volumes live.

searchDocs.title: Simple Use​
searchDocs.content: Create and play a looping sound using the SoundEntity API:  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local endingMusic = VluxySF.Create("MUSIC/LoFi/beat1"):SetLooped(true):Play()

searchDocs.title: _Preload Startup Example​
searchDocs.content: Sometimes you need a sound to be loaded on startup or the first time it’s played. There is no difference in fetching the sound besides the extra child location.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local endingMusic = VluxySF.Create("SFX/Guns/_Preload/shotgunFired1"):PlayOnce()

searchDocs.title: _Preload SoundEntity Example​
searchDocs.content: Sometimes you don’t know if something needs to be preloaded until the game starts. In that case, you can preload your fetched sound manually:  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() -- Preload as soon as possible local endingMusic = VluxySF.Create("MUSIC/LoFi/beat1"):Preload() -- yields -- do stuff here endingMusic:Play() -- this sound plays first try

searchDocs.title: Yielding on a Separate Thread​
searchDocs.content: If you want to deal with sounds on a separate thread, you can use Fork. To clean up any forks, use RemoveForks.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local endingMusic = VluxySF.Create("MUSIC/LoFi/beat1"):Fork(function(self) self:Sleep(10):Play() -- sleep method waits for 10 seconds end)   This waits for 10 seconds and then plays the sound on a separate thread.

searchDocs.title: SoundSchema​
searchDocs.content: Schema </> interface SoundSchema { UnattachedSounds: Configuration -- A folder for storing unattached sounds. SoundGroupContainer: Configuration -- A folder for storing sound groups. SoundGroups: SoundGroups-- A dictionary of sound group instances, organized by name. SoundDefinitions: SoundDefinitions-- A dictionary of sound definitions, organized by sound name. DefinitionsGroupHash: Dictionary<Dictionary<string>>-- Set of hashmaps for fast searching. PreloadIds: {string}-- A list of sound asset IDs to preload. } Defines the overall schema for the sound system, containing all necessary data for sound creation and management.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinitionInstanced​
searchDocs.content: Instanced </> type SoundDefinitionInstanced = Sound & SoundChildrenInstanced Defines a sound group, which is a collection of sounds that can be managed together, allowing for collective volume control and organization.

searchDocs.title: SoundChildrenInstanced​
searchDocs.content: Instanced </> interface SoundChildrenInstanced { TremoloSoundEffect: TremoloSoundEffect? ReverbSoundEffect: ReverbSoundEffect? PitchShiftSoundEffect: PitchShiftSoundEffect? FlangeSoundEffect: FlangeSoundEffect? EqualizerSoundEffect: EqualizerSoundEffect? EchoSoundEffect: EchoSoundEffect? DistortionSoundEffect: DistortionSoundEffect? CompressorSoundEffect: CompressorSoundEffect? ChorusSoundEffect: ChorusSoundEffect? } Defines the possible child sound effects that can be attached to a sound instance, allowing for enhanced audio manipulation and variation.

searchDocs.title: SoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: Instanced </> type SoundGroups = {[string]: SoundGroup } Defines the type of a sound group instance.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type SoundDefinitions = {[string]: SoundDefinition} Defines the sound definitions of the system as a dictionary of sound names to their corresponding sound data.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinition​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> interface SoundDefinition { Properties: SoundSerialized-- The properties of the sound itself, such as SoundId, Volume, etc. Effects: SoundChildrenSerialized-- The sound effects of the sound, organized by class name. } Defines the properties and sound effects of a sound.

searchDocs.title: SoundChildrenSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> interface SoundChildrenSerialized { ChorusSoundEffect: ChorusSoundEffectSerialized? CompressorSoundEffect: CompressorSoundEffectSerialized? DistortionSoundEffect: DistortionSoundEffectSerialized? EchoSoundEffect: EchoSoundEffectSerialized? EqualizerSoundEffect: EqualizerSoundEffectSerialized? FlangeSoundEffect: FlangeSoundEffectSerialized? PitchShiftSoundEffect: PitchShiftSoundEffectSerialized? ReverbSoundEffect: ReverbSoundEffectSerialized? TremoloSoundEffect: TremoloSoundEffectSerialized? } Defines the sound effects of a sound as a dictionary of class names to their corresponding properties.

searchDocs.title: SoundSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type SoundSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of a sound as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: ChorusSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type ChorusSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of a ChorusSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: CompressorSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type CompressorSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of a CompressorSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: DistortionSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type DistortionSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of a DistortionSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: EchoSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type EchoSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of an EchoSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: EqualizerSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type EqualizerSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of an EqualizerSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: FlangeSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type FlangeSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of a FlangeSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: PitchShiftSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type PitchShiftSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of a PitchShiftSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: ReverbSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type ReverbSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of a ReverbSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: TremoloSoundEffectSerialized​
searchDocs.content: Serialized </> type TremoloSoundEffectSerialized = {[string]: any} Defines the properties of a TremoloSoundEffect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: IsInitialized​
searchDocs.content: Startup </> VluxySF.IsInitialized() → boolean-- Whether the sound schema has been initialized. Checks if the sound schema has been initialized. if Sounds.IsInitialized() then print("Sound schema is ready to use!") else print("Sound schema is not initialized yet. Please wait!") end

searchDocs.title: WaitForSchema​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields Startup </> VluxySF.WaitForSchema() → boolean-- Whether the sound schema is ready for use. Waits until the sound schema is initialized and ready for use. If you know that your code will execute after the schema is loaded then this is not needed. print("Waiting for sound system to initialize...") Sounds.WaitForSchema() print("Sound system is now initialized and ready to use!") --now you can use functions and methods that require the schema!

searchDocs.title: GetSoundDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: DefinitionData RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundDefinitions() → SoundDefinitions-- All converted sound data Returns all serialized sound data. local definitions = Sounds.GetSoundDefinitions() for location, data in pairs(definitions) do print("Sound location:", location) print("Sound properties:", data.Properties) print("Sound effects:", data.Effects) end

searchDocs.title: GetSoundLocations​
searchDocs.content: DefinitionData RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundLocations() → {string}-- All registered sound locations Returns all sound locations. local locations = Sounds.GetSoundLocations() for _, location in ipairs(locations) do print("Sound location:", location) end

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: SoundGroups RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroups() → SoundGroups-- All sound groups defined in the schema. Returns all sound groups defined in the schema.

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroup​
searchDocs.content: SoundGroups RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroup( name: string-- The name of the sound group to retrieve. ) → SoundGroup? -- The sound group instance if found, otherwise nil. Retrieves a sound group instance by its name.

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroupNames​
searchDocs.content: SoundGroups RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroupNames() → {string}-- A list of all sound group names defined in the schema. Returns a list of all sound group names defined in the schema.

searchDocs.title: SetSoundGroupVolumes​
searchDocs.content: SoundGroups RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.SetSoundGroupVolumes( volumes: {[string]: number}-- A dictionary mapping sound group names to their desired volume levels. ) → () Sets the volume levels for multiple sound groups at once based on the provided dictionary of group names and volume values.

searchDocs.title: Fetch​
searchDocs.content: Fetcher RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.Fetch( location: string-- The direct location of the SoundDefinition or the name of the SoundDefinition. ) → Sound? -- The fetched sound instance if found, otherwise nil. Finds the specified SoundDefinition and gives back a playable Sound if it exists.

searchDocs.title: FetchFromGroup​
searchDocs.content: Fetcher RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.FetchFromGroup( groupName: string,-- The name of the sound group to search within. name: string-- The name of the SoundDefinition to find within the specified sound group. ) → Sound? -- The fetched sound instance if found, otherwise nil. Similar to Fetch, but is more accurate than fetching by name but less accurate then the full location. A middle ground so you can write less and still somewhat specify!

searchDocs.title: FetchPlayOnce​
searchDocs.content: Fetcher RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.FetchPlayOnce( name: string,-- The name of the sound definition to fetch. parent: Instance? ,-- Optional parent instance to which the sound will be attached before playing. waitOnLoad: boolean?-- Whether to wait for the sound to load before playing (default: false). ) → Sound? -- The fetched sound instance if found, otherwise nil. Fetches a sound, sets it up for one-time playback, and optionally waits for it to load before playing. The sound will be automatically cleaned up after it finishes playing.

searchDocs.title: ToDefinition​
searchDocs.content: Conversions </> VluxySF.ToDefinition( sound: Sound -- The Sound instance to convert. ) → SoundDefinition-- The resulting SoundDefinition based on the properties and effects of the provided Sound instance. Converts a Roblox Sound instance into a SoundDefinition.

searchDocs.title: FromDefinition​
searchDocs.content: Conversions </> VluxySF.FromDefinition( definition: SoundDefinition-- The SoundDefinition to convert. ) → Sound -- The resulting Sound instance based on the properties and effects defined in the provided SoundDefinition. Converts a SoundDefinition back into a playable Roblox Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: PreloadSelected​
searchDocs.content: Preload </> VluxySF.PreloadSelected( ...: string | Sound | SoundDefinition-- A variable number of sound identifiers, which can be sound locations, Sound instances, or SoundDefinitions. ) → () Preloads through ContentProvider.

searchDocs.title: GetPlayLength​
searchDocs.content: Misc </> VluxySF.GetPlayLength( sound: Sound -- The sound instance to analyze. ) → number-- The estimated play length of the sound, including any variations in playback speed. Calculates the estimated play length of a sound instance by considering its SoundId and any attached sound effects that may influence its duration. local soundInstance: Sound = --[[ reference to an existing Sound instance ]] local estimatedLength = Sounds.GetPlayLength(soundInstance) print("Play length:", estimatedLength, "seconds")

searchDocs.title: Jitter​
searchDocs.content: Jitter </> VluxySF.Jitter( center: number,-- The central value around which the jitter will occur. spread: number-- The maximum deviation from the center value. ) → () → number-- A function that generates a random jitter value when called. Jitter function for sound properties.

searchDocs.title: JitterRange​
searchDocs.content: Jitter </> VluxySF.JitterRange( min: number,-- The minimum value for the jitter range. max: number-- The maximum value for the jitter range. ) → () → number-- A function that generates a random jitter value within the specified range when called. Jitter function that generates a random value within a specified range.

searchDocs.title: JitterScale​
searchDocs.content: Jitter </> VluxySF.JitterScale( spread: number-- The maximum percentage deviation from the base value (e.g., 0.1 for ±10% jitter). ) → () → number-- A function that generates a random jitter multiplier when called. Jitter function that generates a random multiplier around 1, allowing for percentage-based variation.

searchDocs.title: JitterUnitLog​
searchDocs.content: Jitter </> VluxySF.JitterUnitLog( center: number,-- The central value around which the jitter will occur. spread: number-- The maximum deviation from the center value. ) → () → number-- A function that generates a random jitter value with a shaped distribution when called. Jitter function that produces values with a shaped distribution, creating more natural variations around the center value.
